subject: SCIENCE
title: Identify objects, activities, and natural events observed
objective: Identify objects, activities, and natural events observed in the local environment that involve science.
introduction: Did you know you're a scientist? 🧪 Every time you ask 'why is the sky blue?' or 'how do plants grow?', you're doing science! Science is about observing the world, asking questions, and discovering answers. Today you'll explore how science is hiding in your kitchen, your garden, your playground — everywhere!

vocabulary.definition: Panonood nang mabuti gamit ang iyong mga pandama.

activities.instruction: Watch and discover — science is hiding all around you!
activities.content: When you cook rice, you're doing CHEMISTRY! When you water a plant and it grows, that's BIOLOGY! When you slide down a slide, that's PHYSICS — gravity pulls you down! When you see a rainbow after the rain, that's light science. Science isn't just in books — it's in your kitchen, your garden, and your playground!

feedback.correct: Now you see — science is everywhere!

activities.accessibilityAlternative: Science is in cooking (chemistry), gardens (biology), playgrounds (physics), and the sky (light and weather).

activities.instruction: Tap each scene to discover the science hiding there!

examples: Kitchen 🍳 — When water boils and turns into steam, that's science! Cooking food changes it — that's chemistry!
Garden 🌱 — Seeds grow into plants using sunlight. Butterflies change from caterpillars — that's a life cycle!
Playground 🛝 — You slide down because gravity pulls you. You swing back and forth because of motion!
Sky 🌈 — Rainbows appear when sunlight shines through raindrops. Clouds form from water vapor — that's weather science!

feedback.correct: You found science in all four places!
feedback.retry: Tap each scene — there's science in all of them!

activities.accessibilityAlternative: Four scenes: kitchen (chemistry), garden (biology), playground (physics), sky (light/weather).
activities.prompt: Where is it? Tap to find!
activities.narration: Find it in the picture! Tap where you think it is.

activities.instruction: Sort each activity: Is it SCIENCE or NOT SCIENCE?

fits: Watching a caterpillar become a butterfly
Mixing vinegar and baking soda to see bubbles
doesNotFit: Watching TV without thinking about how it works

feedback.correct: Yes! Science is about observing and testing, not just guessing.
feedback.retry: Think: does it involve observing or testing something?

activities.accessibilityAlternative: Sort activities into 'uses scientific thinking' or 'does not use scientific thinking'.
activities.prompt: Sort them into the right boxes.
activities.narration: Let's organize! Which items go where?

activities.instruction: You see water boiling in a pot. What kind of science is this?

options: Chemistry — the water is changing form!

feedback.correct: Right! Water changing from liquid to gas is chemistry in action.
feedback.retry: Think about what's happening to the water.

activities.accessibilityAlternative: Choose the type of science involved in boiling water.
activities.narration: Which one is correct? Think about what we learned!

activities.instruction: Match each place to the science you can find there!

feedback.correct: You matched them all! Science really is everywhere.
feedback.retry: Match each place to the kind of science you do there.

activities.accessibilityAlternative: Match locations to the type of science activity that happens there.
activities.narration: Every pair has a match! Can you find them all?

activities.instruction: A scientist follows steps. Put these in the right order!

steps: Test your guess and see what happens!

feedback.correct: That's the scientific method! Observe, Ask, Guess, Test!
feedback.retry: What does a scientist do first?

activities.accessibilityAlternative: Order the steps of the scientific method: Observe, Question, Hypothesize, Test.
activities.narration: Arrange them step by step! Start from the beginning.

items.question: What science topic did we explore?

choices.text: How things work in our world

items.narration: Milo asks: What science topic did we explore?

items.question: What does a scientist do?

choices.text: Observe, ask questions, and test ideas

items.narration: Milo asks: What does a scientist do?

items.question: Where can you find science?

choices.text: Everywhere — at home, in nature, in you!

items.narration: Milo asks: Where can you find science?

items.question: What is the first step in exploring?
items.narration: Milo asks: What is the first step in exploring?

items.question: Why is science exciting?

choices.text: You discover amazing things about the world!

items.narration: Milo asks: Why is science exciting?

assessment.narration: Milo says: Great job! Now let's see what you remember!

storyIntro: Milo looked outside and saw something amazing. "Let's explore and discover!"
